summary: Operational Monitoring & Alerting System - Complete E2E Testing. Backend: 31/31 tests pass (100%). Frontend: All UI elements functional including System Health Overview, 5 domain status cards, Active Alerts with Ack/Resolve buttons, and Detailed Metrics panel. Monitoring worker running with 60s interval. Real provider API integrations in place (HotelRunner REST + Exely SOAP).

minor.issue: Returns 500 - unrelated to monitoring, pre-existing issue

critical_code_review_comments: Monitoring router correctly implements all 6 endpoints with proper auth
Alert engine evaluates 14 threshold types across 5 health domains
Auto-resolve mechanism works when conditions normalize
Worker state correctly exposed via get_monitoring_worker_state()
Frontend Monitoring tab displays all required components: health overview, 5 domain cards, alert list with actions, detailed metrics
Snapshot collectors upgraded from mocked stubs to real HotelRunnerProvider and ExelyClient classes
Ingest pull workers use real provider API clients for hotelrunner and exely
seed_data_creation: No new seed data - tested with existing system alerts generated by monitoring worker
context_for_next_testing_agent: Operational Monitoring System fully tested. All 6 API endpoints working (overview, alerts, metrics, providers, ack, resolve). Frontend at /data-model → Monitoring tab displays: system health score, 5 domain cards, provider health breakdown, active alerts with Ack/Resolve buttons, detailed metrics panel. Monitoring worker runs every 60s creating alerts based on thresholds. ARI Push currently 'critical' status due to 0% success rate (no recent pushes). Snapshot collectors and ingest workers upgraded to use real HotelRunner and Exely API clients.

monitoring: GET /api/channel-manager/monitoring/overview — 200 OK (system_health, providers, active_alerts, critical_alerts, queue_depth, reconciliation_open_cases, 5 domain statuses, worker state)
GET /api/channel-manager/monitoring/alerts — 200 OK (alerts list with filtering by status/severity/provider)
GET /api/channel-manager/monitoring/metrics — 200 OK (all 5 health domains: provider_health, ingest_health, ari_health, reconciliation_health, queue_health)
GET /api/channel-manager/monitoring/providers — 200 OK (provider health breakdown with provider-specific alerts)
POST /api/channel-manager/monitoring/alerts/{id}/ack — 200 OK (acknowledges active alert, 404 for nonexistent, 400 for already resolved)
POST /api/channel-manager/monitoring/alerts/{id}/resolve — 200 OK (resolves alert, 404 for nonexistent, 400 for already resolved)

system_health_overview.summary_text: Shows providers count, active alerts count, open cases count

Monitoring Tab.quick_stats: Active Alerts, Critical Alerts, Queue Depth, Open Cases

detailed_metrics_panel.ingest_pipeline_metrics: Total Events, Pending, Failed 24h, Duplicate Rate
detailed_metrics_panel.ari_push_engine_metrics: Pushes 24h, Success Rate, p50/p95 Latency, Pending Sets
detailed_metrics_panel.reconciliation_metrics: Open Cases, Critical, New 24h, Cases by type

monitoring_worker_verified.creates_alerts: Based on 14 threshold evaluations across 5 health domains
monitoring_worker_verified.auto_resolves: When conditions normalize, alerts are auto-resolved

ingest_workers.hotelrunner_pull: Uses HotelRunnerProvider for real API calls
ingest_workers.exely_pull: Uses ExelyClient for real SOAP calls

real_provider_integrations.note: Actual provider API credentials may not be configured in test environment, so real API calls may fail gracefully
